reasoning:
The question requires assessing whether ACLED-recorded fatalities in Cambodia for the 30 days before March 8, 2026 (roughly Feb 6 - Mar 7) will exceed 10 times a baseline derived from the previous year. The minimum possible threshold for resolution (assuming the baseline remains exactly 1.0) is strictly greater than 10 fatalities (i.e., 11 or more in a 30-day period). If the baseline updates to incorporate the dozens of fatalities from the July and December 2025 border clashes with Thailand, the threshold would be substantially higher (e.g., 35+ fatalities). 

Exhaustive searches of news covering Cambodia from late January through the end of February 2026 yield no reports of significant violence, political unrest, or deadly border clashes. A ceasefire agreement between Thailand and Cambodia took effect in early January 2026 and reports from mid-to-late February confirm the truce has broadly held without any new mass fatalities. In the absence of any major deadly incidents in February, and with only one week remaining in the 30-day target window, the likelihood of Cambodia experiencing 11+ fatalities (let alone potentially dozens if the baseline is revised) is extremely minimal.
